...

paragraphs preempting the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient defense (paragraph 1: three-demand rebuttal of the retreat to a phenomenological coefficient) and the annual-aether-circulation escape (paragraph 2: equivalence to heliocentric kinematics + dome-ontology incompatibility + WIN-017 Gaia cross-WIN handcuff).

current_field_values.detail_evidence_first_chars: James Bradley (1728) explicitly tested and rejected atmospheric refraction as an explanation: refraction is wavelength-
current_field_values.detail_evidence_last_chars: incompatible with Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry by more than six orders of magnitude.
current_field_values.annual_periodicity_anchor_in_detail_evid: Independently, stellar aberration varies sinusoidally with a precise one-year period.

replacement_detail_extra:
A dome defender's strongest available move is to retreat from the refractive-medium framing entirely: declare α = 2.56×10⁻⁸ a phenomenological deflection coefficient with no claim about an underlying physical refractive index, and the Kramers-Kronig objection never bites. The retreat is real — KK constrains causal physical n(ω), not arbitrary fitting parameters. But the move is from explanation to description, and three demands the dome model still owes survive untouched: (i) derivation — no published expression connects α to disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), sun_altitude (5,733 km), or any other dome parameter; raw-text/ search returns zero non-trivial mentions, and the numerical proximity to 2.59·(v/c)² looks like reverse-engineering against the textbook constant rather than a derivation; (ii) periodicity — a fitted constant cannot produce the empirically observed annual sinusoidal variation of stellar aberration any more than a refractive index can; the dome must declare α time-varying with a one-year phase, which is geometrically equivalent to reintroducing Earth's orbital motion under a different name; (iii) achromaticity — the same fitted constant would have to hold to milliarcsecond precision across six orders of magnitude in frequency (VLBI ∼10⁹ Hz vs Gaia optical ∼5·10¹⁴ Hz), which is exactly the empirical signature of a non-dispersive cause (orbital velocity), not a tunable parameter of any medium-or-deflection model. The phenomenological-coefficient retreat removes one objection; it does not address any of the three the dome registry's CONFIRMED status would need to honour.

An obvious follow-up escape — declaring α itself annually time-varying via some aetheric circulation — collapses on contact with dome ontology. An aether circulation with a precise one-year period whose phase tracks Earth's orbital position is, up to relabelling, the heliocentric explanation: the same v(t)/c modulation the standard model attributes directly to Earth's orbital velocity. Adopting it concedes the kinematic content of heliocentrism while refusing the name. It is also incompatible with the dome's published static-disc, fixed-firmament foundation: the v51.1 registry, the model.html parameter list, and the v48.0 evolution notes (where the WIN-016 entry first appeared) do not describe α as time-varying or invoke any annual aether dynamics. The empirical handcuff is even tighter when paired with WIN-017: both WINs anchor on Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry, with WIN-017 (Refuted by Data — parallax ∝ 1/distance) and WIN-016 (here) using the same dataset against the dome model from independent geometric directions. Any escape that disqualifies Gaia to save one breaks the other; any escape that accepts Gaia leaves both intact. The defense's three-from-three structural failure plus the cross-WIN data handcuff together reduce the steelmanned defense_survives from 3/5 to roughly 1/5: the only remaining residue is unfalsifiable "future dome version will derive α from geometry and explain its annual phase" hand-waving, which is exactly the post-hoc carve-out our Part 3.1b structural test is built to refuse.
holes_addressed: ISS-1838 / WIN-016.c3 advocate (defense_survives=3): the dome defender's strongest move is the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat — declare α = 2.56×10⁻⁸ a phenomenological deflection coefficient with no claim about an underlying physical refractive index, and Kramers-Kronig (a constraint on causal physical n(ω)) literally does not apply. The current detail_evidence text frames α as a refractive index throughout; a defender pointing this out wins a real but narrow rhetorical concession. The inserted paragraph 1 closes this loophole by acknowledging the retreat is real (KK is a physical constraint, not a constraint on arbitrary fitting parameters) while showing that three independent demands the dome registry's CONFIRMED status owes — derivation from dome geometry, mechanism for annual periodicity, explanation for radio-optical achromaticity across 10⁶× in frequency — survive untouched by the move. Paragraph 2 closes the natural follow-up escape (declare α annually time-varying via aetheric circulation): such a circulation is geometrically equivalent to heliocentric orbital kinematics under a different name, is incompatible with the dome's published static-disc / fixed-firmament foundation (v48.0 evolution notes, model.html parameter list, v51.1 registry all describe α as a static constant), and any data-trust escape is structurally handcuffed to WIN-017 because both WINs depend on the same Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry from independent geometric directions. After integration, defense_survives should drop to 1–2 (residual: only "future dome version will derive α and explain its annual phase" hand-waving remains — which is exactly what Part 3.1b post-hoc test refuses).
new_evidence_added: Acknowledgment that Kramers-Kronig is a constraint on causal physical refractive indices n(ω), not on phenomenological deflection coefficients in general (Toll 1956, standard dispersion-relation derivations). This is the technical concession the dome defender correctly extracts; the new text grants it explicitly so the rebuttal cannot be undermined by a 'you are misrepresenting KK' counter.

Geometric-equivalence argument for annually-time-varying α: a periodic aether circulation tracking Earth's orbital phase reproduces the same v(t)/c modulation the standard model attributes to Earth's orbital velocity — a Newtonian-frame relabelling, not a distinct mechanism. (Standard kinematics; see e.g. Misner-Thorne-Wheeler chapter on relativistic frames for the equivalence-of-relabellings discussion.)

Cross-WIN handcuff: WIN-017 (Gaia DR3 parallax ∝ 1/distance, Refuted by Data) and WIN-016 (here) both anchor on Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry from independent geometric directions; any escape that disqualifies Gaia for one breaks the other. (Gaia Collaboration 2023, A&A 674 A1; reference target identified by curmudgeon WIN-016.c3 stronger_arguments[0].)

Quantitative anchor for the retreat-doesn't-help-on-derivation claim: the numerical proximity α ≈ 2.59 · (v/c)² (with v/c = 9.93×10⁻⁵) is documented in the curmudgeon review's quantitative_verification block; the text cites it as evidence the value was reverse-engineered against the textbook constant rather than derived from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude.

anticipated_objections.objection: You say α cannot be a fitted parameter without forfeiting the CONFIRMED status, but every physical model has fitted parameters — G in Newtonian gravity, e in QED. Why is the dome held to a different standard?
anticipated_objections.response: G and e are single fitted parameters that, once fixed, predict an open-ended set of independent observations (planetary motions, atomic spectra, scattering cross-sections, etc.) across many orders of magnitude in scale. They earn their parameter status by predictive surplus. α = 2.56×10⁻⁸ in the dome registry is fitted to one observation (the 20.5 arcsec annual aberration) and predicts no other independent measurement; the dome registry lists no second use of α, no derived consequence in any other WIN, no chromatic / temporal / cross-frequency prediction that could falsify it independently. A fitted parameter that explains exactly one number it was tuned against is curve-fitting, and Part 3.1b is exactly the post-hoc test built to surface this pattern (see WIN-016 detail_verdict_text for the standing rider). The double standard is real but runs the other direction: G and e satisfy a discipline α does not.

anticipated_objections.objection: Paragraph 2 dismisses the annually-circulating-aether escape as 'geometrically equivalent to heliocentric kinematics,' but kinematic equivalence is not the same as ontological equivalence. The dome could accept that α(t) modulates with annual phase while still rejecting the heliocentric ontology — the equivalence is up to coordinate choice.
anticipated_objections.response: The dome's claimed differentiator from heliocentrism is not coordinate-choice; it is the physical static-disc / fixed-firmament structure. An aetheric circulation with a one-year period and a phase locked to Earth's orbital position is itself a non-static feature of the firmament — it is precisely the kind of dynamic the dome's foundational documents (v48.0 evolution notes; model.html) reject. Adopting α(t) does not preserve "static dome" while sacrificing "heliocentric kinematics"; it sacrifices the static dome to recover the kinematics. The dome may make that trade, but the v51.1 registry has not, and until it does, the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat remains incompatible with the model the registry actually publishes.

anticipated_objections.objection: The WIN-017 cross-handcuff over-commits. WIN-017 uses parallax-distance scaling, WIN-016 uses aberration-velocity scaling; a defender could accept Gaia data for one geometric purpose and reject it for the other (cite e.g. systematic errors in different astrometric pipelines).
anticipated_objections.response: Gaia DR3 produces a single self-consistent astrometric solution per source; parallax and proper motion (which encodes aberration corrections) are jointly fitted in the same five-parameter astrometric model (right ascension, declination, parallax, proper motion in RA, proper motion in dec). Selectively rejecting one component while accepting the other is not a cited systematic in the Gaia DR3 validation papers; the components are coupled at the catalogue-construction level. A defender can postulate uncited systematics, but doing so against the Gaia validation literature is itself a moveable goalpost — the same Part 3.1b structural concern recurs at the meta level.

kernel_of_truth: The dome defender has identified a genuine technical gap in the original critique. The Kramers-Kronig dispersion relations are a constraint on causal physical refractive indices n(ω) derived from microscopic medium response — they are NOT a constraint on arbitrary phenomenological deflection coefficients in the abstract. If α = 2.56×10⁻⁸ is interpreted as a fitted bending parameter in some ad-hoc deflection formula, with no claim that it arises from a dispersive medium, the KK objection literally does not bite the dome's text — it bites only the strawman version where α is treated as a Maxwell-equation refractive index. The defender is correct that the original critique took the strongest interpretation against the dome (treat α as a real n) without citing any dome text that says α IS a refractive index in the physical-medium sense. Why this still does not save the WIN: the move from physical refractive index to phenomenological deflection coefficient is a retreat from explanation to description. The dome registry's CONFIRMED status is supposed to mean the dome model PREDICTED the observation from dome physics, not that the dome adopted a number to match it. Once α is just a fitted constant with no mechanism, the registry status loses content: derivation, periodicity, achromaticity remain unanswered — and Part 3.1b structural test was built to refuse exactly this kind of post-hoc demotion. So the kernel — 'KK applies to physical n, not to fitted constants' — is correct, and the retreat dodges KK; but the retreat dodges KK by sacrificing the predictive content the WIN's CONFIRMED status was supposed to embody.

Paragraph 2's annual-circulation closure complements but does not duplicate it: detail_evidence states the periodicity argument cleanly; detail_extra closes the specific aether-circulation escape from it.
No changes to detail_verdict_text — the Part 3.1b post-hoc rider stands.
No changes to claim, finding, verdict, detail_claim, detail_group, code_analysis, new_in_v51.

EXP-286 was created with the defense-neutralization scope only; these other holes warrant separate EXP entries or a follow-on minor_edit pass by the decider.
After integration, recommend curmudgeon re-review WIN-016 (cycle 4) and downgrade defense_survives 3 → 1 or 2; close ISS-1838 referencing this EXP.
